steps.title: Make two points for the line
steps.content: The line passes through $(0,80)$. Its slope is $-3$, so when $x$ increases by $1$, $y$ decreases by $3$. That gives a second point, $(1,77)$.

steps.title: Fit the points in Desmos
steps.content: Enter $x_1=[0,1]$ and $y_1=[80,77]$, then run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=-3$ and $b=80$, so the line is $y=-3x+80$.

steps.title: Identify the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = -3x + 80$': correct slope and $y$-intercept. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = -3x + 77$': subtracts 3 from the $y$-intercept instead of using it directly. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = -3x - 77$': wrong sign and wrong intercept value. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = -3x - 80$': negates the $y$-intercept. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
